channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Was Anyone at Fault in Sierra Nevada Avalanche Deaths?
item.description: Few laws govern backcountry skiing in California. Experts say that people engage in the sport at their own risk, but guides have responsibilities to k...

item.title: What worked and what didn't with a cellphone ban at a Kentucky school
item.description: Keeping students off their devices is the new norm in many schools. We talked to students and educators at one Kentucky school to see how it's working...

item.title: Israeli settlers kill 19-year-old Palestinian American, officials and witnesses say
item.description: Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank shot and killed a Palestinian American man during an attack on a village, the Palestinian Health Ministry s...

item.title: Trump says he doesn't know if aliens are real but directs government to release files on UFOs
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he's directing the Pentagon and other government agencies to release files related to extraterrestrials and ...

item.title: Inside Trump’s ‘Royal Court’
item.description: The Atlantic staff writers Ashley Parker and Michael Scherer explain how Trump’s White House works.

item.title: Another Winter Storm Could Hit the Northeast This Weekend
item.description: There’s a lot of hype, but not a lot of certainty among forecasters yet. Here are three scenarios to watch.

item.title: Scientists worry about lasting damage from Potomac sewage spill
item.description: Drinking water around the District of Columbia hasn't been contaminated. But scientists say the environmental damage could be severe.

item.title: Using saliva to detect disease holds promise, but it's not perfected yet
item.description: Easier than a blood test, saliva tests have the potential to detect cavities, infections and even cancer. But a lack of insurance coverage and other o...

item.title: A Once Prominent American Statesman Faces Fallout From the Epstein Files
item.description: George Mitchell, a former Senate majority leader credited with bringing peace to Northern Ireland, is under renewed scrutiny over his association with...

item.title: Mixed Signals Churn Stock Market Despite Recent Highs
item.description: Major indexes have mostly been treading water for months, as investors’ exuberance for artificial intelligence wears off.

item.title: Olympics Gold Medalist Eclipsed By Her Toddler.
item.description: A champion speedskater spoke to reporters with her toddler. The focus on her child was among several instances at the Olympics that highlighted the co...

item.title: A New U.S. Blockade Is Strangling Cuba
item.description: An analysis of ship movements shows that the Trump administration is isolating the island at one of its most vulnerable moments.

item.title: Austrian Man Found Guilty in Girlfriend’s Death on Mountain Hike
item.description: Thomas Plamberger was given a suspended prison sentence and fined in a case that tested the limits of personal responsibility in mountaineering.

item.title: How China Is Stoking Fear About Travel to Japan
item.description: From earthquake warnings to bear attack alerts, Beijing is deploying a campaign of exaggeration and disinformation to punish Tokyo’s support for Taiwa...

item.title: A Makeup Ban in Syria for Women in Government and Public Sector Stirs Outrage
item.description: A provincial governor wants to prohibit women in government and public sector jobs from wearing makeup, stirring outrage and many, many memes.
